name: Ration Card (Food Security Card)
issuingAuthority: Civil Supplies Department (Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Affairs), Government of Telangana
dataSource: civilsupplies.telangana.gov.in and epds.telangana.gov.in — official Telangana government websites

sections.purpose: A Ration Card (also called Food Security Card / FSC) is issued by the Civil Supplies Department of Telangana. It entitles eligible households to: (1) Subsidised food grains (rice) from Fair Price Shops (FPS) under the Public Distribution System (PDS). (2) Access to other welfare commodities at subsidised prices. (3) Serves as identity and address proof for government services. In Telangana, ration cards are Aadhaar-seeded and authentication is done biometrically at FPS shops. Source: civilsupplies.telangana.gov.in
sections.eligibility: Categories of ration cards in Telangana (as per scribd/MeeSeva Citizen Charter): Yellow Ration Card (Priority Household — PHH): for BPL/poor families eligible under NFSA. Saffron/Orange Card (Non-Priority): for other eligible families. White Card: issued to APL households. Eligibility criteria: Must be a permanent resident of Telangana. Household should not already hold a ration card in any state. Income and asset criteria as per state/national guidelines. All family members must have Aadhaar for seeding. Source: civilsupplies.telangana.gov.in
documents: Application form (available at MeeSeva centre or online at epds.telangana.gov.in)
Aadhaar cards of ALL family members (mandatory — for Aadhaar seeding with ration card)
Address proof: Electricity bill / Property Tax Receipt / Voter ID (showing Telangana address)
Income certificate (required for BPL/PHH category — issued by Revenue Department/Tahsildar)
sections.fees: New ration card issuance fees (as per Telangana MeeSeva Citizen Charter / scribd official document): Yellow Ration Card (PHH/BPL): ₹5. Saffron Ration Card: ₹20. White Ration Card (APL): ₹50. MeeSeva service charge: nominal (₹10–₹35). No government fee beyond card issuance charge. Source: MeeSeva Citizen Charter for Telangana
sections.processingTime: New ration card: 30 to 45 days after field verification by Area Inspector, Civil Supplies. Addition of new member: 7–15 days. Corrections: 15–30 days. Name removal: 7–15 days. Source: civilsupplies.telangana.gov.in / MeeSeva Citizen Charter
sections.validity: Ration cards do not expire but are subject to periodic verification. All members must have valid Aadhaar seeding for continued benefits. Members who are no longer eligible (e.g., income above threshold, moved permanently) should be removed to avoid cancellation of the entire card.
applicationSteps: Step 1 — Visit nearest MeeSeva centre or epds.telangana.gov.in
Step 2 — Obtain and fill the prescribed application form with all family member details (names, Aadhaar numbers, income details)
Step 3 — Attach Aadhaar copies of ALL family members
Step 4 — Submit address proof, income certificate (for BPL), and head of family photograph
Step 5 — Pay the applicable card issuance fee (₹5/₹20/₹50 depending on category)
Step 6 — Note the Application Reference Number
Step 7 — Area Inspector / Civil Supplies Officer visits household for field verification
Step 8 — After approval, ration card issued within 30–45 days
Step 9 — Check FSC/ration card status at epds.telangana.gov.in → FSC Application Search
sections.addMember: To add a new member (newborn/newly married): Visit MeeSeva with existing ration card, Aadhaar of new member, and birth certificate (for newborn) or marriage certificate (for spouse). Processing: 7–15 days. Source: MeeSeva Citizen Charter
sections.removeMember: To remove a deceased or permanently moved member: Submit death certificate (for deceased) or surrender/migration form at MeeSeva. Keeps remaining family entitlement intact. Source: MeeSeva Citizen Charter
sections.lostCard: If ration card is lost: Apply for duplicate at MeeSeva or Civil Supplies office. Nominal fee. Your entitlement continues — you can collect food grains from FPS using Aadhaar biometric authentication even without physical card (Aadhaar-based authentication is active at FPS in Telangana). Source: civilsupplies.telangana.gov.in
sections.tracking: Check ration card status: Visit epds.telangana.gov.in → FSC Application Search → Enter Reference Number or Aadhaar Number. Also check: epds.telangana.gov.in → FSC Search. Civil Supplies helpline: 1967. Source: epds.telangana.gov.in
commonMistakes: Not seeding all family members' Aadhaar — biometric authentication at FPS fails; food grains cannot be collected. Source: civilsupplies.telangana.gov.in
Applying while already holding a ration card in another state — this leads to cancellation
Not providing income certificate for BPL/PHH category — application rejected
Not removing deceased/migrated members — may trigger verification issues for remaining family
Providing wrong household income — leads to wrong category assignment or rejection

edgeCases.title: Aadhaar not seeded — biometric authentication fails at FPS
edgeCases.problem: Family has ration card but cannot collect food because Aadhaar is not seeded or biometric authentication fails at Fair Price Shop.
edgeCases.recommendedNextStep: Visit nearest Civil Supplies office or MeeSeva for Aadhaar seeding of affected members. If biometric failure: update biometrics at Aadhaar Seva Kendra first. Alternatively, use OTP-based authentication if mobile is linked to Aadhaar. Source: civilsupplies.telangana.gov.in

faqs.question: What is ration card in Telangana?
faqs.answer: A Ration Card (Food Security Card) issued by Telangana Civil Supplies Department entitles eligible families to subsidised food grains from Fair Price Shops under PDS. Also serves as identity and address proof. Source: civilsupplies.telangana.gov.in

faqs.question: Who is eligible for ration card in Telangana?
faqs.answer: Permanent residents of Telangana without a ration card in any other state. Categories: Yellow (BPL/PHH), Saffron, White (APL). All family members' Aadhaar must be seeded. Source: civilsupplies.telangana.gov.in

faqs.question: What documents are needed for ration card?
faqs.answer: Aadhaar of all family members, address proof, income certificate (for BPL), head of family photograph, application form. Source: MeeSeva Citizen Charter, civilsupplies.telangana.gov.in

faqs.question: What is the fee for ration card in Telangana?
faqs.answer: Yellow card (BPL): ₹5. Saffron card: ₹20. White card (APL): ₹50. Plus nominal MeeSeva service charge. Source: MeeSeva Citizen Charter, Telangana

faqs.question: How to apply for ration card in Telangana?
faqs.answer: Visit MeeSeva or epds.telangana.gov.in. Fill form with all family Aadhaar details. Submit documents. Field verification by Civil Supplies Inspector. Card issued in 30–45 days. Source: civilsupplies.telangana.gov.in

faqs.question: How to add new member to ration card?
faqs.answer: Visit MeeSeva with existing ration card, new member's Aadhaar, and birth/marriage certificate. Processing: 7–15 days. Source: MeeSeva Citizen Charter

faqs.question: How to check ration card status in Telangana?
faqs.answer: Visit epds.telangana.gov.in → FSC Application Search → Enter Reference Number or Aadhaar. Civil Supplies helpline: 1967. Source: epds.telangana.gov.in

faqs.question: What if I cannot collect food — biometric failure?
faqs.answer: Visit Civil Supplies office or MeeSeva for Aadhaar re-seeding. Update biometrics at Aadhaar Seva Kendra if fingerprints are degraded. Use OTP authentication if mobile is Aadhaar-linked. Source: civilsupplies.telangana.gov.in
